California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Peter A. Bagatelos 
Bagatelos & Fadem 
601 California st., Ste. 1801 
San Francisco, CA 
94108 
Dear Mr. Bagatelos: 
March 1, 1989 
Re: 
Your Request for Informal 
Assistance 
Our File No. 1-89-060 
You have requested confirmation of my telephone advice 
regarding entities which fail to file reports for entire years. 
Your summary of my advice is correct to the extent that it 
applies to the discussion I recall. 
That is, a current recipient 
committee recently discovered that it should have begun filing 
reports as a recipient committee in 1986 but had not begun filing 
as a recipient committee until 1987. 
I advised you that the 
committee could file one report for all of 1986, rather than 
filing separate reports for the semi-annual and pre-election 
reporting periods. 
Please note that this advice does not apply to the most 
recent previous year's campaign disclosure statements. 
For 
example, if a committee discovered in 1989 that it had failed to 
file reports for all of 1987 and all of 1988, it would be 
acceptable to file one report covering all of 1987, but separate 
semi-annual and pre-election reports would be required for 1988. 
·1 hope this information is helpful. 
If you have additional 
questions, please call me at (916) 322-5662. 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
By: 
Carla Warldow 
Political Reform Consultant 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660 


LAW OFF'CES OF 
BAGATELOS & FADEM " .. , 
BARRY FADEM 
PETER A. BAGATELOS 
WES VAN WINKLE 
Ms. Carla Wardlow 
THE INTERNATIONAL BUILD'NG 
60' CALIFORNIA STREET 
SUITE IBOI

SAN FRANCISCO, CALlFORN",l~J"'OB

Sacramento, California 
95814 
Dear Carla:

t.

TELEPHONE 
'4IS) 9B2'7'OO 
FAX 
("'5) 9B2 'OBS 
This will confirm our telephone conversation of January 23, 
1989. 
I asked you if it has been the practice of your office to 
give advice that entities which fail to file campaign reports 
for one or more years, who subsequently determine that they had 
to file statements, could file a single report covering a whole 
calendar year instead of ~eparate reports. 
You advised me that it has been standard advice for as long 
as you can remember, while working in the technical assistance 
office of the Fair Political Practices commission, that 
organizations finding themselves in such a situation could file 
one statement covering an entire year, with all disclosable 
information, instead of having to file separate reports that 
• might otherwise have been required for separate segments of the 
calendar year. 
I mentioned to you that Rich Eichmann told me that he had 
received this advice from you on several occasions in the past, 
and I wanted to confirm that this has been standard advice. 
As 
you recall, I used an example of a recipient committee for 1986 
that determines in a subsequent year that it should have filed 
reports as a recipient committee. 
In this context, you 
confirmed that the advice of your office has been that it would 
be permissiblE! for such an organization to file a single report 
for the entire year, disclosing all pertinent information. 
Thank you very much for your assistance and advice. 
PAB/ki

I asked you if it has been the practice of your office to 
give advice that entities which fail to file campaign reports 
for one or more years, who subsequently determine that they had 
to file statements, could file a single report covering a whole 
calendar year instead of separate reports. 
You advised me that it has been standard advice for as long 
as you can remember, while working in the technical assistance 
office of the Fair Political Practices Commission, that 
organizations finding themselves in such a situation could file 
one statement covering an entire year, with all disclosable 
information, instead of having to file separate reports that 
might otherwise have been required for separate segments of the 
calendar year. 
I mentioned to you that Rich Eichmann told me that he had 
received this advice from you on several occasions in the past, 
and I wanted to confirm that this has been standard advice. 
As 
you recall, I used an example of a recipient committee for 1986 
that determines in a subsequent year that it should have filed 
reports as a recipient committee. 
In this context, you 
confirmed that the advice of your office has been that it would 
be permissible for such all organization to file a single report 
for the entire year, disclosing all pertinent information. 
Thank you very much for your assistance and advice. 
PAB/ki
